# typespacing

> Adjust per-character kerning in the browser, export as JSON, apply in production.

## Install

```bash
npm install typespacing
```

## API

Single public entry point: `createKerningEditor(options?)`

```ts
import { createKerningEditor } from 'typespacing'
```

### Options

```ts
interface KerningEditorOptions {
  editable?: boolean      // true = editing UI, false = apply only (default: true)
  kerning?: KerningExport // kerning data to apply on mount
  locale?: 'en' | 'ja'   // UI language (default: 'en')
}
```

### Edit (development / staging)

```ts
const editor = createKerningEditor({ editable: true })
editor.mount()
```

Pass `kerning` to resume editing from a previous export:

```ts
const editor = createKerningEditor({ editable: true, kerning: previousData })
editor.mount()
```

### Apply (production)

```ts
import kerningData from './kerning-export.json'

const editor = createKerningEditor({ editable: false, kerning: kerningData })
editor.mount()
```

### Events

```ts
editor.on('enable', () => { /* editing mode activated */ })
editor.on('disable', () => { /* editing mode deactivated */ })
editor.on('change', ({ selector, kerning, indent }) => { /* value changed */ })
editor.on('select', ({ selector, gapIndex, gapIndexEnd }) => { /* selection changed */ })
editor.on('reset', () => { /* all kerning cleared */ })
```

`on()` returns a dispose function:

```ts
const off = editor.on('change', handler)
off()
```

### Lifecycle

```ts
editor.mount()    // attach to DOM (waits for DOMContentLoaded if needed)
editor.unmount()  // detach from DOM, clean up all listeners
```

## Kerning data format

```ts
interface KerningExport {
  version?: number
  exported: string
  page: string
  areas: Array<{
    selector: string
    text: string
    font: { family: string; weight: string; size: string }
    indent?: number
    kerning: number[]  // 1/1000em units per character gap
  }>
}
```

## Editor shortcuts

- Cmd/Ctrl + K: toggle edit mode
- Click: select text block and gap
- Shift + Click: extend selection to range
- Tab / Shift+Tab: next / previous gap
- Arrow Left/Right: move cursor
- Shift + Arrow Left/Right: extend selection
- Arrow Up/Down: move vertically
- Alt + Arrow Left/Right: adjust by +/-10
- Alt + Cmd/Ctrl + Arrow Left/Right: adjust by +/-100
- Esc: clear selection
- B: toggle Before/After compare

When multiple gaps are selected, Alt + Arrow adjusts all at once (tracking).

## HTML attributes

- `data-typespacing-ignore`: add to any element to exclude it from editing

## Key facts

- Zero dependencies, framework-agnostic
- Spacing via margin-left on per-character spans (not letter-spacing)
- Preserves inline markup (<em>, <strong>, <span>)
- Kerning values are in 1/1000em (integer). 100 = 0.1em
- kerning[i] = gap after character i. indent = gap before first character
- mount() automatically waits for DOM ready if called early
- Target elements should be block-level (<p>, <div>, <h1>), not <span>
